executive_summary: Iteration 000033 delivers all 5 image-generation pipelines (SDXL T2I, SDXL T2I Refiner Prompt, SDXL Turbo, Z-Image Turbo, Anima Preview), three new node wrappers (empty_sd3_latent_image, sd_turbo_scheduler, sample_custom_simple), five example scripts, and six pytest test files. All 134 tests pass on CPU. The implementation is structurally complete and correct with one minor gap: the Z-Image Turbo pipeline (z_image/turbo.py) omits the optional per-file path-override keyword arguments required by FR-5, using hardcoded manifest-derived filenames instead.

verification_by_fr.notes: sampling.sd_turbo_scheduler(model, steps, denoise=1.0) wraps SDTurboScheduler; lazy import confirmed; present in __all__.

sampling.sample_custom_simple(model, add_noise, noise_seed, cfg, positive, negative, sampler, sigmas, latent_image) wraps SamplerCustom; returns output latent at index 0; lazy import confirmed; present in __all__.
latent.empty_sd3_latent_image(width, height, batch_size=1) wraps EmptySD3LatentImage logic via torch + comfy.model_management; lazy import confirmed; present in __all__.
All five pipeline run() functions call check_runtime() first and raise RuntimeError with the error message if an error key is present. Pattern consistent across all modules.

SDXL T2I, SDXL T2I Refiner Prompt, SDXL Turbo, and Anima T2I expose per-file path-override keyword arguments (base_filename, refiner_filename, ckpt_filename, unet_filename, clip_filename, vae_filename). Z-Image Turbo (z_image/turbo.py) does NOT expose any path-override kwargs; it always resolves paths from manifest() entries only, giving callers no way to override individual model file names at call time.
All pipeline modules live under comfy_diffusion/pipelines/image/ as specified.
__init__.py files present at: pipelines/, pipelines/image/, pipelines/image/sdxl/, pipelines/image/z_image/, pipelines/image/anima/.

verification_by_us.notes: t2i.py exists; manifest() returns 2 correct checkpoints; run() signature matches; KSamplerAdvanced (via sample_advanced) with correct add_noise/return_with_leftover_noise/start_at_step/end_at_step settings; refiner VAE used for decode; lazy imports; check_runtime() call; __all__ and docstring present.

t2i_refiner_prompt.py exists; manifest() identical to US-001; run() adds refiner_prompt and refiner_negative_prompt kwargs (default to base prompt/negative when None); refiner CLIP used for refiner-stage conditioning; two-pass KSamplerAdvanced flow identical to US-001; lazy imports; __all__ and docstring present.

turbo.py exists; manifest() returns 1 entry (sd_xl_turbo_1.0_fp16.safetensors); run() accepts all required args; euler_ancestral via get_sampler(); sd_turbo_scheduler(model, steps=1, denoise=1.0); sample_custom_simple() used; lazy imports; __all__ and docstring present.

z_image/turbo.py exists; manifest() returns 3 correct entries; run() accepts models_dir, prompt, width, height, steps, seed (no negative_prompt); load_clip with clip_type='lumina2'; model_sampling_aura_flow(shift=3); empty_sd3_latent_image(); conditioning_zero_out(); sampler res_multistep, scheduler simple, cfg 1.0; lazy imports; __all__ and docstring present.

anima/t2i.py exists; manifest() returns 3 correct entries; run() accepts all required args; load_clip with clip_type='stable_diffusion'; empty_latent_image(); er_sde sampler; cfg defaults 4.0, steps defaults 30; lazy imports; __all__ and docstring present.

All three wrappers added: latent.empty_sd3_latent_image (latent.py), sampling.sd_turbo_scheduler and sampling.sample_custom_simple (sampling.py). All present in __all__. All use lazy imports. All wrap correct ComfyUI internals.

All 5 example scripts exist: sdxl_t2i.py, sdxl_refiner_prompt_t2i.py (includes --refiner-prompt), sdxl_turbo.py, z_image_turbo.py (no --negative-prompt, consistent with pipeline), anima_preview.py. All use argparse, download_models(), run() pattern consistent with existing examples.

All 6 test files present: test_pipelines_image_sdxl_t2i.py, test_pipelines_image_sdxl_refiner_prompt.py, test_pipelines_image_sdxl_turbo.py, test_pipelines_image_z_image_turbo.py, test_pipelines_image_anima_t2i.py, test_image_wrappers_it033.py. Each covers structure, __all__, manifest, run signature, lazy imports, and mock-based run(). test_image_wrappers_it033.py covers all three new wrappers. All 134 tests pass on CPU (uv run pytest).

minor_observations: z_image/turbo.py uses hardcoded _UNET_DEST.name, _CLIP_DEST.name, _VAE_DEST.name constants inside run() instead of exposing optional path-override kwargs (unet_filename/unet_path, clip_filename/clip_path, vae_filename/vae_path). The other four pipelines expose filename overrides for flexibility.
sample_custom_simple() uses 'noise_seed' as parameter name in the public signature, while the PRD spec mentions the same parameter as 'noise_seed'. This is consistent — no mismatch.

SDXL Turbo run() defaults cfg=0.0 (turbo effectively bypasses CFG), which is correct for the distilled model but differs from the PRD's US-003-AC03 which does not specify a default. This is a sensible implementation choice.

pipelines/__init__.py defines __all__ = ['video'], reflecting previous iteration's video pipelines. The new image sub-package is accessible via explicit import but not re-exported from the top-level pipelines package — consistent with the project's explicit submodule import convention.
conclusions_and_recommendations: Iteration 000033 is nearly fully compliant. All user stories comply and six out of seven functional requirements fully comply. The single partial-compliance gap is FR-5: z_image/turbo.py does not expose optional path-override kwargs, making it impossible for callers to swap individual model files without modifying the pipeline. Recommended action: add unet_filename, clip_filename, and vae_filename optional keyword arguments to z_image/turbo.py run() with defaults derived from manifest(), matching the pattern used by the other four pipelines. This is a small, low-risk change that can be addressed in a refactor pass or carried as minor technical debt if the Z-Image model layout is considered stable.
